reasoning:
The resolution criteria ask if the highest-grossing film worldwide in 2025 will be part of a franchise. The base rate for this is extremely high (~90%). Looking at the 2025 slate, the leading contender to top the global box office in late 2025 is standard franchise fare: James Cameron's 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' (Avatar 3), which is tightly scheduled for December 2025 and strongly predicted to make over $1.5B+.

But more significantly, a movie released in early 2025 has already set an impossibly high floor. The Chinese animated blockbuster 'Ne Zha 2', which is fundamentally a franchise film (a sequel to the 2019 mega-hit 'Ne Zha'), released in late January 2025 and has already amassed $2+ billion globally at the box office (as of late March 2025) surpassing even 'Avengers: Infinity War' and 'Inside Out 2' on the all-time charts. 

Therefore, any original movie hoping to take the #1 spot in 2025 would need to grossly exceed $2 billion dollars worldwide — a feat achieved by fewer than 5 films in cinema history, and never by an original film (except the first Avatar). So the only film capable of beating 'Ne Zha 2' for the 2025 crown is 'Avatar 3', which is also a franchise. This essentially locks in the result. I am assigning 0.98, the highest permitted probability.
